OpenCloud Web allows developers to implement apps for viewing and editing specific file types. For instance, the built-in preview app serves as the default application for opening media files like images, videos, or audio. This section will guide you through the process of implementing such an app within OpenCloud Web.

An app is essentially a distinct package that must be specified as an external application in the Web configuration. The structure of an app is quite simple and straightforward. Consider, for example, the pdf-viewer app. It consists of a package.json file, a src directory containing all the source code, and a l10n directory for translations. Optionally, you may also include a tests directory if your application requires testing. To learn more about apps in general, please refer to the Web app docs.

Inside the src folder you will need an index.ts file that sets up the app so it can be registered by the Web runtime. It follows the basic structure as described in the apps section, so it may look like this: import { AppWrapperRoute, defineWebApplication, AppMenuItemExtension } from '@opencloud-eu/web-pkg'; import translations from '../l10n/translations.json'; import { useGettext } from 'vue3-gettext'; import { computed } from 'vue'; // This is the base component of your app. import App from './App.vue'; export default defineWebApplication({ setup() { // The ID of your app. const appId = 'advanced-pdf-viewer'; const { $gettext } = useGettext(); // This creates a route under which your app can be opened. // Later, this route will be bound to one or more file extensions. const routes = [ { name: 'advanced-pdf-viewer', path: '/:driveAliasAndItem(.*)?', component: AppWrapperRoute(App, { applicationId: appId }), meta: { authContext: 'hybrid', title: $gettext('Advanced PDF Viewer'), patchCleanPath: true } } ]; // if you want your app to be present in the app menu on the top left. const menuItems = computed<AppMenuItemExtension[]>(() => [ { label: () => $gettext('Advanced PDF Viewer'), type: 'appMenuItem', handler: () => { // do stuff... } } ]); return { appInfo: { name: 'Advanced PDF Viewer', id: appId, defaultExtension: 'pdf', extensions: [ // This makes sure all files with the "pdf" extension will be routed to your app when being opened. // See the `ApplicationFileExtension` interface down below for a list of all possible properties. { extension: 'pdf', routeName: 'advanced-pdf-viewer', // Add this if you want your app to be present in the "New" file menu. newFileMenu: { menuTitle() { return $gettext('PDF document'); } } } ] }, routes, translations, extensions: menuItems }; } }); Here is the interface defining the extensions property of the appInfo object. interface ApplicationFileExtension { app?: string; extension?: string; createFileHandler?: (arg: { fileName: string; space: SpaceResource; currentFolder: Resource; }) => Promise<Resource>; hasPriority?: boolean; label?: string; name?: string; icon?: string; mimeType?: string; newFileMenu?: { menuTitle: () => string }; routeName?: string; }
After installing the app, you can now set up your OpenCloud account.
When you first start the OpenCloud Android app, you will see the following screen. To set up an additional account, click on the 'Manage accounts' button and select 'Add account'.
Enter your OpenCloud Server URL (e.g., https://cloud.example.com) Tap ">" to continue.

The Login Page will be opening and you musst type in your username and password. Then tap "Log in". In the following screen, grant the app permission to access your user information and maintain continuous access by tapping the "Allow" button.
Once login is complete, your account will be added to the app Your OpenCloud account is now fully set up and ready to use!
In the Android app settings changes can be made, such as Security Manage notifications Logging Advanced settings and more

Collabora Online allows you to to create / edit documents (text, spreadsheets, presentations) directly in your browser without any additional software. The integration with OpenCloud provides seamless access to your files and enables real-time collaboration.
All documents stored in OpenCloud can be opened directly in Collabora.

Performance depends on your internet connection and the size of the document. For advanced features or detailed instructions, we refer to the official Collabora Online documentation.

Here you enter a name for the shortcut with which it will be displayed
To create the shortcut, click on "Create"
Your created shortcut is now displayed and can be used
Symbolic links (symlinks) are not synchronized by the OpenCloud Desktop Client. Users often wonder why linked folders or files are missing or not accessible.

Symlinks are deliberately excluded from synchronization for several important reasons: Not portable: Symlinks often point to paths that only exist on the original machine. On another device, the target path likely doesn't exist. Not usable in the web interface: The web interface cannot interpret or display symlinks. Problematic on Windows: Symlink support on Windows is limited and inconsistent. Risk of circular references: Symlinks could point to each other in loops, causing infinite synchronization cycles. Loss of identity: If the client were to follow the link and synchronize the target, it would become a regular copy of the data. The original nature of the symlink would be lost.

Sync Folders Outside the Sync Root Using Symlinks If you want to synchronize a folder that is located outside your sync root, you can still achieve this by moving the folder into your sync root and replacing the original location with a symlink. Example​ You want to synchronize the folder /foo/A, but your sync root is /home/bar/OpenCloud/Personal. Move the folder into your sync root: mv /foo/A /home/bar/OpenCloud/Personal/foo/A

The OpenCloud Desktop Client does not synchronize files starting with ~$, such as ~$document.docx. These files are temporary lock files created by Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) while a document is open. They are not actual content files but serve as internal markers to prevent multiple users from editing the same document simultaneously. Once the file is closed, the ~$ file is automatically removed by Office. For more details, see Microsoft’s explanation: Description of how Word creates temporary files
documents.s: Files with "~$" in the name will not be synchronized

documents.t: The OpenCloud Desktop Client enables file synchronization between a local computer and OpenCloud. It ensures that files are available offline and that changes are automatically synchronized once an internet connection is restored. The client is available for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Key Features​ Synchronization of files and folders between desktop or laptop computer and OpenCloud Automatic updates when changes occur Offline access to important data

When files are modified both locally and remotely before synchronization, the Desktop Client creates "conflicted copy" files—examples include: conflict.txt (remote version) conflict (conflicted copy YYYY-MM-DD HHMMSS).txt (local version) This usually happens when: Your local copy and the server copy diverge simultaneously, and the app cannot automatically merge changes.
documents.s: Handle file conflicts in the OpenCloud Desktop Client

documents.t: In the Desktop Client overview, a notification will appear when conflicting files are detected. This means that multiple versions of the same file exist—often because changes were made on different devices before synchronization. The notification serves as a prompt for you to review the conflicts and decide how to merge or keep the versions.

To resolve a conflict: Open both files (the original and the conflicted copy). Manually compare and merge the differences. Edit the base file (conflict.txt) to include both sets of changes if needed. After merging, delete the conflicted copy file. Leave the updated base file—sync will now continue as normal.
Avoid editing the same file simultaneously on multiple devices. Always wait for sync completion before making local changes.

The Desktop Client determines a conflict when: Local and remote versions of a file both change since the last sync. Content is compared using file IDs and checksums—not only timestamps. The client preserves both versions by renaming the local file with "(conflicted copy ...)". Conflict files are created locally; they do not appear on the server unless explicitly uploaded. If you're experiencing frequent conflicts—especially on simple edits—it’s likely due to permission issues, read-only files, or syncing the same files via other tools. Check logs and file attributes accordingly.

When using the OpenCloud Desktop Client, file and folder names must meet certain operating system (OS) requirements to ensure seamless sync across different platforms. These restrictions are not enforced by OpenCloud but originate from system limitations.

Avoid using prohibited characters or reserved words in filenames on any OS. If syncing from Linux/macOS to a Windows-based share, make sure filenames are compatible with Windows naming restrictions. To change casing (e.g., File.txt → file.txt) on Linux/macOS when syncing to Windows, rename the file to a completely new name, let it sync, then rename it to the desired casing.

Windows limits file paths to 260 characters by default. If your sync root exceeds this, the Desktop Client will warn: "The path 'YOUR.LONG.PATH' is too long. Please enable long paths in the Windows settings or choose a different folder." On Windows 10 and later, you can remove this limit by enabling Long Paths. See Microsoft’s documentation.
Linux/macOS: NUL (character code 0) Windows: ASCII 0 – 31 While valid on some systems, such characters often cause issues during syncing.
Avoid using any of the following as filenames: CON, PRN, AUX, NUL, COM1–COM9, LPT1–LPT9
On Linux/macOS syncing to SMB, filenames that differ only by case may cause conflicts—rename files clearly to avoid sync failures. Windows: Filenames must not end in a space or period (.).

Creating a file named example. or example.LPT1 on macOS may sync to OpenCloud successfully. However, when accessed via a Windows client, these files may be rejected due to reserved naming or format rules, leading to inconsistent sync behavior across devices.

Restriction Type Action Item Path Length Keep paths under ~260 characters unless extended Forbidden Characters Remove disallowed characters from names Control Characters Avoid non-printable ASCII characters Reserved Filenames Do not use Windows reserved names Case-Only Changes Rename to a temporary name before syncing Trailing Characters Avoid filenames ending in space or period
If you need to generate logs for troubleshooting, follow these steps:
documents.s: Logging to a Temporary Directory in OpenCloud Desktop

documents.t: Click on Settings → Advanced → Log Settings
In the Log Output window, check the box for Enable logging to temporary folder To help the Support-Team and the developers, it is helpful to enable the Log Http traffic
Click Open folder to access the logs. Select the log files for the time frame when the issue occurred. note These logs can help diagnose and fix any issues with your OpenCloud Desktop Client.

25-02-17 09:02:35:174 [ info sync.httplogger ]: REQUEST 3710cc12-7391-4793-8e89-00499dc11983 {"request":{"body":{"length":0},"header":{"accept":"*/*","accept-language":"en_DE","original-request-id":"3710cc12-7391-4793-8e89-00499dc11983","user-agent":"Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh) mirall/1.0.0-git (OpenCloud, macos-24.3.0 ClientArchitecture: x86_64 OsArchitecture: arm64)","x-request-id":"3710cc12-7391-4793-8e89-00499dc11983"},"info":{"cached":false,"id":"3710cc12-7391-4793-8e89-00499dc11983","method":"GET","url":"https://cloud.opencloud.test/.well-known/openid-configuration"}}} Log Content Description 25-02-17 09:02:35:174 Timestamp of the request [ info sync.httplogger ] Log category label 3710cc12-7391-4793-8e89-00499dc11983 X-REQUEST-ID (used to match requests & responses) Header: List of HTTP headers Data: [] HTTP bodies (JSON, XML) (112ms) Response time (since the request was sent)

The OpenCloud desktop app sends an X-REQUEST-ID header with every request. This ID helps in finding corresponding requests and responses in logs. You can configure your web server to add the X-REQUEST-ID to its logs for deeper analysis. note This feature is useful for debugging sync issues, monitoring network activity, and troubleshooting connectivity problems.
The OpenCloud Desktop Client is available as an AppImage for Linux. Using AppImageLauncher is recommended to ensure proper system integration, menu entries, and simplified updates.
AppImageLauncher integrates AppImages into your system and manages them like regular applications. Follow the installation instructions here: AppImageLauncher Installation Guide
Download the latest or desired .AppImage version from the official release page: OpenCloud Desktop Releases on GitHub Save the file to your preferred download directory.

Once AppImageLauncher is installed: Right-click the downloaded OpenCloud.AppImage. Select “Open with AppImageLauncher”. Confirm integration when prompted. AppImageLauncher will automatically: store the AppImage in the correct location register a menu entry ensure the app behaves like a native application

After integration, you can start the client as usual through your application menu: open your Application Launcher search for OpenCloud Desktop start the application The client will guide you through the initial setup.
When a new version is available, download the updated AppImage from GitHub. AppImageLauncher will detect it and offer to replace the existing version automatically.

To remove the OpenCloud Desktop Client: Open your application menu. Search for OpenCloud Desktop Select the option to remove or uninstall the AppImage (menu wording may vary depending on distribution and launcher). AppImageLauncher will cleanly remove the integrated AppImage.
You can synchronize multiple accounts from different OpenCloud servers to your local machine. This allows you to manage files from different instances within the same desktop client.
documents.s: Using Multiple Accounts in OpenCloud Desktop

documents.t: Open OpenCloud Desktop Click on Add Account Follow the standard setup process: Enter the server URL Log in with your credentials Authorize access
documents.s: How to Add a New Account

documents.t: After adding multiple accounts, they will appear in the account overview in the settings menu You can easily switch between them to manage synchronization settings for each account separately

In your File Explorer, each account will have a separate folder By default, the folders are named: OpenCloud (for the first account) OpenCloud (2) (for the second account) OpenCloud (3) (for the third account), and so on note This setup ensures that files from different accounts remain organized and do not mix.

To remove the OpenCloud Desktop Client from your system: Open the Applications folder. Locate OpenCloud Desktop.app. Drag the application to the Trash or right-click and select Move to Trash. Empty the Trash to complete the removal. Optional configuration files may remain in the user directory: ~/Library/Application Support/OpenCloud/ ~/Library/Preferences/eu.opencloud.desktop.plist ~/Library/Logs/OpenCloud/ These files can be deleted manually if a full cleanup is desired.

Click "Open web browser" to log in automatically Alternatively, copy the provided URL and paste it into your browser manually
Enter your username and password Click "Login"
Accept the access request to link your account with OpenCloud Desktop.

Choose whether to sync all files or select specific folders (this can be adjusted later). Change the local download directory where your files will be stored. Once you're ready, click Done to complete the setup. OpenCloud Desktop is now successfully set up. You can start syncing your files seamlessly between your device and the OpenCloud server!
The OpenCloud Desktop Clientfor Windows is available through the Microsoft Store. Using the Store ensures a simple installation process, automatic updates, and seamless integration with Windows.
Open the Microsoft Store on your Windows device and search for OpenCloud Desktop, or click the button below to go directly to the app page:

On the app page, select Install to download and install the OpenCloud Desktop Client. The Microsoft Store will handle the installation automatically and place the application in the appropriate location on your system.

Once the installation is complete, launch the client by opening the Start Menu and searching for OpenCloud Desktop. The application will guide you through the initial setup, including signing in with your account.

The Microsoft Store keeps the OpenCloud Desktop Client up to date. Updates are installed automatically in the background and require no manual steps. This ensures: the latest features are always available security improvements are applied promptly full compatibility with current Windows versions note Installing from the Microsoft Store ensures automatic updates, improved system integration, and reduces the need for manual maintenance.

If you ever need to uninstall the client: Open the Start Menu. Search for OpenCloud Desktop. Right-click the application and select Uninstall. Windows will remove the app and its components cleanly through the Microsoft Store framework.
Follow these simple steps to install and configure your OpenCloud Desktop and start syncing your files effortlessly.
Open your OpenCloud Desktop Enter the URL of your OpenCloud instance Click "Continue"
Click "Open web browser" to log in automatically Alternatively, copy the provided URL and paste it into your browser manually
Enter your username and password Click "Login"
Accept the access request to link your account with OpenCloud Desktop.

Choose whether all existing Spaces should be synchronized or should the synchronization be configured manually. Change the local download directory where your files will be stored. Once you're ready, click Done to complete the setup. OpenCloud Desktop is now successfully set up. You can start syncing your files seamlessly between your device and the OpenCloud server!
You can customize OpenCloud Desktop settings by following these steps:
Click on "Settings" in the top-right corner of OpenCloud Desktop
Choose to sync hidden files Decide whether deleted files should be moved to the local trash bin Edit the ignored files list Enable a debug log file for troubleshooting
Select how the client handles proxy settings: No proxy Use system proxy (default) Manually enter a proxy
Set bandwidth limits: No limit (default) Automatically adjust limit Set a specific limit manually
To find out who has edited a file or folder and what actions have been carried out, follow these steps:
documents.s: Viewing the activities of a file or folder

documents.t: Open details Right-click on the file or folder, or click on the three dots in the corresponding line. Select Details from the context menu. Select activities In the right sidebar opens a window, in this select the “Activities” item. View activities All activities carried out with this file or folder are now displayed. info This allows you to track all changes and actions!
You can easily create new text, table and presentation files in the web interface. You can also rename files and folders or move them to another location

Click on the “+ New” button at the top of the bar. In the menu, select either “Folder” or the desired "file type" that you want to create. Enter a name for the file or folder in the window that appears. Click on “Create”. Done! The file or folder is created. If it is a file, it is automatically opened in edit mode.

Open the context menu by either: - right-clicking on the file or folder, - or clicking on the three dots on the right in the corresponding line. Select the “Rename” option. Enter the new name for the file or folder in the window that appears. Confirm by clicking on “Rename”. Done! - the new name is applied!

Make a selection: - Select the file(s) or folder(s) you want to move. Open the context menu: - Right-click on the file or folder, - or click on the three dots in the corresponding line. Select the “Cut” option from the menu. (Alternatively, you can use the “Cut” function in the action bar at the top). Select destination: - Navigate to the folder or location where the file or folder should be moved to. - Click on “Paste here” in the action bar. The file or folder is now moved to the new location and no longer exists in the original location.

The OpenCloud Desktop Client uses the Windows Virtual File System (VFS) to integrate your cloud data directly into the File Explorer. Files and folders appear in the system without needing to be fully downloaded, saving local disk space.
Files and folders can appear in different states, depending on whether they are stored locally or only available online.
Stored locally and always accessible offline. Windows will not remove the file automatically.
documents.s: Always available on this device (full pinned)

documents.t: Downloaded and accessible offline. Can be removed by Windows if disk space is needed. Newly created or added files automatically receive this state.
Shown in File Explorer but stored only in the cloud. Downloaded automatically when opened; requires an internet connection.
To store a file, folder, or Space locally: Right-click the item in File Explorer. Select “Always keep on this device”.
To remove local copies while keeping them visible in File Explorer: Right-click the item. Select “Free up space”.
Online-only items are downloaded automatically when opened. Changes are synced back to OpenCloud. note An internet connection is required for online-only files.

Select the file(s) or folder(s) you wish to delete. "Right-click" on the selection, - or click on the "three dots" in the line of the file/folder. Select the “Delete” option in the menu. info Alternatively, you can also click on “Delete” in the action bar. The files or folders are moved to the “Deleted files” area. They can be restored from there if necessary.

Navigate to “Deleted files” Click on “Deleted files” in the left sidebar. Files from your personal space can be found under “Personal”. Files from other spaces (e.g. “Space 1”) can be found in their respective spaces. Click on the file(s) or folder(s) you want to restore. Make a right-click, - or click on the three dots next to the selection. info Alternatively, you can also click on “Restore” in the action bar. note The files or folders are restored to their original location.
With OpenCloud you can create shortcuts to websites or files

Click on “+ New” in the batch actions and select “Shortcut”. Insert link or file: In the window that opens, enter the link or the name of the file you want to link. Alternatively, you can select the desired file from the list after you have entered the first few letters. Specify the name of the shortcut: Enter a name for the shortcut. Create shortcut: Click on “Create” to save the shortcut.
Now click on the shortcut to open the linked web page or file in a new browser tab. or a "Right-click" and "Open shortcut" on it to open the linked web page or file in a new browser tab.
With OpenCloud you can add tags to files and folders to make them easier to search or group.
documents.s: Add and manage tags with OpenCloud

documents.t: Open the context menu. Right-click on the file or folder, or click on the three dots in the corresponding line. Select details Select the “Details” option in the context menu.
Click in the input field next to “Tags”. Select an existing tag, or enter a new term to create a new tag. The tag is added and is now linked to the file or folder.
Open the “Details” of the file/folder again. Find the tag you want to remove. Click on the “x” next to the tag to delete it. The file or folder is now no longer associated with this tag.

The Web Client is the primary interface of OpenCloud. It provides full access through the browser without requiring any additional software installation. Key Features​ File Management: Create, upload, edit, share, and organize files and folders Space and User/Group Management Collabora Online for real-time collaborative document editing
Overview of the web user interface
Change file list views in OpenCloud
OpenCloud saves different versions of files. This allows you to restore or download previous versions:
documents.s: Managing versions of files in OpenCloud

documents.t: Open details Right-click on the file or click on the three dots in the corresponding file line. Select “Details” from the context menu. Select versions In the right sidebar that opens select the “Versions” item. Show versions All versions of the file are displayed. You can now restore or download the desired version. These steps give you access to older versions of your files at any time!
Clicking on the settings icon on the right side of the user interface takes you to the options for customizing the view. Here you can customize the display

Show hidden files Activate or deactivate the display of hidden files. Hidden files are often system or configuration files. Show file extensions Specify whether the file extensions (e.g. .docx, .jpg) should be displayed after the file names. This makes it easier to identify file types. Items per page Specify how many files should be displayed per page in a folder. Useful for navigating in folders with many files. Tile size Customize the size of the icons and buttons: Small tiles for a more compact view. Large tiles for a clearer view.
You can use the search bar in the top bar to find files and content quickly.

The search bar is located at the top of the top bar in the web interface. Click on the drop-down menu next to the search bar. Select whether you want to search in all files or only in the current folder. The Search function searches through file names as well as the content of the files to display relevant results. Use these steps to find your files or folders quickly and efficiently!
documents.s: This is how the search works

documents.t: Click on “Upload” in the action bar. Select whether you want to upload files or folders. Select files/folders: In the window that opens, select the desired files or folders. Several files or folders can be selected at the same time. Confirm upload: Click on “Open” to start the upload. Once completed, a message will appear in the bottom right-hand corner. Alternatively: Drag and drop the files or folders directly from your Explorer window into the browser to upload them.

Select the desired files or folders by either: Clicking in the file line or clicking the box at the beginning of the line. Open the context menu - By right-clicking - or by clicking on the three dots next to the file. - or by clicking the download button in the action bar Select “Download” from the menu.
If you download several files or folders, these are saved as a ZIP file. Unzip the ZIP file on your device after downloading.
The OpenCloud web interface consists of several areas that help you to work efficiently with files and settings
documents.s: OpenCloud - Overview of the web user interface

documents.t: The sidebar contains the navigation elements such as Spaces, Files, Shares and Settings.
The menu in the top left corner allows you to switch between different Apps and modules.
The top bar contains important functions such as the search bar, user actions and global settings.
Allows you to quickly find files, folders or spaces.
Displays all files and folders in a selected view.
Shows the current path of your navigation and allows you to quickly jump back to previous directories.
Allows you to edit, move or delete several selected files at the same time.
Provides various display options for the file list: List view Tile view Condensed view
Here you can customize additional display options such as sorting or column selection.
Click on your profile picture in the top right corner to access Settings, Logout and Account Options.
Shows additional information and panels for shares, activities or file details.
Here you will find Actions such as Download, Share, Delete or Edit for selected files.
Provides further options for files, folders or spaces.
Shows possible actions for the selected file, e.g. rename or move.
Here you can share files and folders internally or externally and manage permissions.
Shows an overview of the versions of a selected file or folder.
Shows an overview of changes and activities within a space or file.
Right-clicking on a file or folder opens the context menu with specific options.
In OpenCloud you have the option of making various settings for your user account to customize it according to your needs
Click on your avatar in the top right corner Select "Preferences"
In the settings you can choose between different sections:
Profile picture Username / Login name First and last name E-mail address Personal storage space Group memberships

In profile picture you can change your Avatar image: Click on "Upload" under your Avatar icon Choose your Avatar image and click on "Upload" now it opens a window you can anjusted your image and then click on the "Set" button after this your Avatar will be set up to your account

Depending on the basic configuration, you can change the following options: Language: Select the desired language for your user interface. Password: Change your password for access to your user account. Theme: Customize the Theme of the user interface according to your preferences. Email notifications: Determine which email notifications you would like to receive from OpenCloud. View options: Set the WebDAV settings to allow external access to files and folders.
Under Extensions you can configure installed extensions, such as the Progressbar, if available.
Under Calendar you can access your personal calendar for integration with third-party apps like Thunderbird, Apple Calendar, and others, when configured.
Under GDPR you can request a data export of your personal data if you wish.
App Tokens allow you to connect external apps and services (such as WebDAV clients) securely without using your main password.

The OpenCloud iOS app provides mobile access to OpenCloud on iPhone and iPad. It offers essential functions for working on the go, including file management, uploads, and quick collaboration. Key Features​ File management: view, upload, share, and organize files and folders Access to spaces and their stored content Full-text and metadata search for quickly finding documents Support for collaborative editing via Web Office Real-time synchronization of changes Use Case: Ideal for users who need mobile access to their OpenCloud data and want to handle essential tasks while on the go.

documents.s: 📄️ Shortcut in the OpenCloud iOS app

documents.t: In OpenCloud you can choose between different view modes for your files and folders to customize the display according to your preferences
documents.s: Change file list views in OpenCloud

documents.t: The classic List view, in which all files and folders are displayed in a detailed table.
A more compact version of the table view, in which the data is summarized more closely to save space.
A view in which files and folders are displayed as tiles. Ideal for visual representations and if you want to access icons quickly.
With the OpenCloud iOS App, you can easily upload files and make them available for offline use.
Navigate to the folder where you want to upload a file.
Tap the “+” icon in the top right corner
From the menu, you can choose an Upload option: "Upload file" – upload a file from your device's Files app "Upload from your photo library" – upload an existing photo or video from your media library
Choose the file or media you want to upload. The upload will start automatically.

You can also make files available offline so they are accessible without an internet connection. Tap the three dots (⋯) next to the file Select “Make available offline” The file will be downloaded and stored locally on your device. Offline files are marked with a cloud icon with a checkmark.
To free up space: Tap the three dots (⋯) again on an offline file Select “Remove offline access” These features ensure you’re always prepared — even when you’re offline!
A short Tutorial how to install the OpenCloud iOS App.
Open the App Store on your iPhone or iPad.
Search for "OpenCloud" or click the following link to go directly to the app: OpenCloud on the App Store
After installation, you will find the OpenCloud app on your home screen.
Open the app and follow the on-screen instructions to set it up.
This section explains the core interface elements of the OpenCloud iOS app and their functionality.
Allows you to go back and forward through previously visited views or folders.
Lets the user search for files, folders, or shared items within the app.
Opens options to upload files, create folders, or add new content.
Displays additional actions or settings related to the current view or item.
Lets the user sort files by name, date, size, or other criteria.
Allows switching between different view modes.
Enables multi-select mode to select multiple files or folders at once.
Shows the contents of the current folder as a list of files and folders.
Displays the current folder path and allows quick navigation to parent folders.
The OpenCloud iOS App makes it easy to manage your content. You can create new folders and files, rename items, and move them to different locations — all directly from your iPhone or iPad.
documents.s: Create, Rename, and Move Files and Folders

documents.t: Tap the “+” button in the top right corner. Choose what you want to create from the menu:
Tap "Create folder" Enter a name for your folder Tap "Done"

If Collabora Online is enabled in your OpenCloud instance: Tap "New document" Choose the document type (text, spreadsheet, presentation) Enter a filename and confirm The document will open in a built-in web view using Collabora for editing.
Select "Take photo or video" from the menu Your device camera will open Capture a photo or video The media will be saved directly to your OpenCloud account

Use your iOS device to scan paper documents: Select "Scan document" Point the camera at your document OpenCloud will auto-detect the edges and scan it You can then: Crop or rotate the scan Switch color modes (color, grayscale, black & white) Choose a file format (PDF, JPEG, PNG) Save as a single-page or multi-page file The scanned document will be saved in your OpenCloud account.

To rename a file or folder in the OpenCloud iOS App: Tap the three dots (⋯) next to the item you want to rename Select "Rename" from the menu Enter the new name for the file or folder Tap "Done" to apply the changes The new name will be saved immediately.

To move a file or folder in the OpenCloud iOS App: Tap the three dots (⋯) next to the file or folder you want to move Select "Cut" or "Copy" from the menu Navigate to the destination folder Tap the three dots (⋯) in the destination location and select "Paste" The file or folder will now appear in the new location.
After installing the app, you can now set up your OpenCloud account.
Tap on "Start Setup" to begin. If you've already set up an account and want to add another one, tap the "+" icon located at the bottom left corner of the screen.
Enter your OpenCloud Server URL (e.g., https://cloud.example.com) Tap "Proceed" to continue.
Tap "Open Login Page" Then tap "Continue" to launch the login page in your browser
Log in with your OpenCloud credentials Grant the app permission to access your user information and maintain continuous access

Once login is complete, your account will be added to the app Optionally, assign a custom display name to your account (By default, it will appear as USERNAME@YOUR.DOMAIN) Your OpenCloud account is now fully set up and ready to use!
In the iOS app settings changes can be made, such as User interface Data usage Advanced settings Media files and more
Here you can choose whether you want to run the app in "light mode" or "dark mode".
Here you can specify where the search function should operate: Folder Tree Space Account Server
Here you can activate and deactivate the logging function and specify the storage location of the log file.
Here you can set whether the app can be used with cellular data or not.
Here you can specify when unused local copies should be deleted.
Here you can enable or disable the display of hidden files and folders.
Here you can set whether the folders should be displayed before the files in the list view.
Here you can disable gesture control to prevent multiple selection and dragging of files and folders.
Here you can select whether the app is allowed to run diagnostics in the background.
Here you can set whether a file should be downloaded directly or streamed.
Here you can set whether certain files should be converted when uploading, such as HEIC to JPEG.
Here you will find links to our documentation page and help links.
Here you can forward a link to download the iOS app.
A full entry on our privacy policy can be found here.
You can find a full entry on our terms of use here.
Parts of the app may use copyrighted material, which is acknowledged here.
Here you can see which app version is being used.
Here we explain how to use the search function in the OpenCloud iOS app
Click on the search icon to open the search function
The search function offers various options that can be used to narrow down your search
You can search for file or folder names in the search bar.

By clicking the option to the left of the search bar, you can specify where the search function should operate. Folder: Searches only in the current folder. Tree: Searches in the current folder and all subfolders within it. Space: Searches the current space, including all folders and subfolders within it. Account: Searches the entire logged-in account, including all spaces, folders, and subfolders. Server: Searches the entire server.
By clicking on the option "Type", you can set which file type should be searched for
By clicking on the option "date", you can set the period in which you want to search
By clicking on the "Size" option, you can set the file size to search for
By clicking on the "circle symbol" with the three dots in it, you can save the current search
This area displays the search results
In the iOS app, the account menu provides access to various personal and sharing-related features.
Opens the main account options and settings.
Shows which user account is currently active.
Gives access to the user's personal files and folders.
Displays files and folders that have been shared with others or received.
Opens a list of collaborative spaces or team folders.
Allows the user to search through files, folders, and content.
Shows a list of recently accessed or modified files.
Displays files that are marked for offline access.
Indicates the current sync or connection status.
Lets the user add a new account to the application.
Opens the general settings and configuration options.
Here we explain how to share files and folders via invitation with other people and groups within your OpenCloud in the OpenCloud iOS app
documents.s: Share files and folders with people and groups via invitation

documents.t: Click the "three-dot menu" next to the file or folder
In the following selection, click on "Share with"
The Share menu opens, where you can use the search bar to search for people or groups you want to invite

Here you can select which permissions people and groups should have: Can View: People can only view and download content, but not edit, create, or upload it. Can Edit: People can view, download, upload, edit, and create files. Custom: Permissions can be individually selected here.
To create the link you must click on "Invite"
Once the invitation is created, you will see it in the file or folder overview
Here we explain how to share files and folders with others via link in the OpenCloud iOS app
documents.s: Share files and folders via link

documents.t: Click the three-dot menu next to the file or folder
In the following selection, click on "Create link"
Here you can select and specify several options for the link to be created
Enter a name for your link here

Here you can select which function your link should have: Invite people: Only invited people have access to the link and its content. Can View: People can only view and download the content of the link, but not edit, create, or upload. Can Upload: People can only view the content of the link and upload files, but not edit or create them. Can Manage: People can only view, download, and upload files, but not edit or create them. Can Edit: People can view, download, upload, edit, and create the content of the link.
You must enter a password here. You can choose your own or have one generated for you
Here you can specify a period of time for which the link should be available
To create the link you must now click on "Share"
Once the link is created, you will see it with the assigned name in the file or folder overview
Here we show you how to create shortcuts in the iOS app
documents.s: Shortcut in the OpenCloud iOS app

documents.t: -A menu opens with options for shortcuts
To create a shortcut to a website, enter the address of the website under "URL"
To create a shortcut to a file or folder in OpenCloud, click the "Select file or folder" option
Here you enter a name for the shortcut with which it will be displayed
To create the shortcut, click on "Create shortcut"
Your created shortcut is now displayed and can be used
The "Spaces" section provides a central place for collaboration in groups or teams. In a Space, multiple users can access files and folders together, organize content, and track changes
documents.s: The "Spaces" Section in the OpenCloud iOS app

documents.t: To open the Spaces Overview, tap the "Spaces button" in the account menu. Afterwards, the Spaces Overview appears, listing all Spaces you are a member of or have created yourself.
By tapping the search icon, the search menue for Spaces will open.
By tapping the plus icon, the available functions within a Space appear.
The add menu in the iOS app allows users to create and upload content directly into a Space.
Create new folders to organize and structure content clearly.
Create a new document directly within the Space without using a separate app.
Select files from your device and upload them to the Space.
Access your iOS photo library and upload images or videos directly to the Space.
Use your iOS camera to take pictures or videos and upload them directly to the Space.
Create a shortcut to a file or folder within the Space for quick access.
Use your device camera to scan physical documents and save them directly as PDFs in the Space.
By tapping the three-dot menu, a pop-up menu opens where you can choose to make the Space available offline.
Role view download upload edit add delete only view doc, img, pdf with watermark can view (secure) - - - - - - x can view x x - - - - - can upload x x x - - - - can edit x x x x x x -
In a Space, members can have different roles that determine what they can do with shared files and folders.
You can only view documents, pictures, and PDFs. A watermark is added to the files. Downloading is not allowed.
You can view and download files and folders. No restrictions on downloading.
You can view, download, and upload files and folders.
You can edit, add, and delete files and folders. Full access to modify shared content. Each role gives a different level of access, ensuring the right permissions for each user!
Role Personal Space create Spaces can be Space Manager manage Users and Groups Admin x x x x Space Admin x x x - User x - x - User Light - - x -

The Administrator role in OpenCloud has the same abilities as a Space Admin but in addition it has special permissions to manage users, groups, and system settings. However, administrators do not have direct access to Space data or the ability to add users to Spaces where they don't have the "can manage" role. Below are the key actions an administrator can perform: Create and Delete local Users & Groups Administrators can add new local users and remove existing local users from the system. They can also create and delete local groups to organize users efficiently. Edit Users & Groups User details such as names, emails, and roles can be modified. Groups can be updated by adding or removing members as needed. Prevent Login If necessary, administrators can disable user accounts, preventing them from logging in. This is useful for security reasons or when an account is no longer needed. Manage Spaces (without Access to Space Data) Administrators can manage Spaces like enable, disable or delete them and rename it or adjust the quota, even if they don't have the "can manage" role. However, they cannot access the files inside a Space or add members to it. Change the Logo The system logo can be updated to reflect the company branding. This can be done in the Admin Settings under the "General" section.

A Space Admin has the highest level of control over Spaces. They can: Create and manage Spaces Be assigned the "Can Edit" role in Spaces they didn’t create Have a personal Space where they can create files, folders, and upload data

A regular User has access to their own Space and can be part of other Spaces. They can: Create files and folders in their personal Space Upload and manage their own data Be added as a member to a Space Can have the "Can Edit" role in a Space

A User Light has limited access and does not have their own personal Space. They can: Be added as a member to a Space Can have the "Can Edit" role in a Space They do not have a personal Space note If a user previously had the role User or higher and is later changed back to User Light, they will still keep their personal Space.
With public links, you can share files and/or folders across organizations.
documents.s: Public links - external sharing of files and folders in OpenCloud

documents.t: Click on the “three dot menu” next to the file or folder name you want to share. Select “Share” from the drop-down menu. A sidebar window will open on the right side of the screen. Scroll down in the sidebar window and click on “Add link”. A pop-up window will open where you can configure the following options:

Select the desired access rights for the recipients: “Can view": The link recipient can view and download the file, but cannot edit or upload files. “Can edit": The link recipient can view, download, edit and delete the file, as well as upload files to the folder. “File Drop (secret)": The link recipient can only upload files, but cannot view or edit the content. Activate the password by entering it in the input field under “Password”. The password requirements appear below the input field. Here are three icons: One icon displays the password unencrypted to verify your entry. The next icon copies the password to the clipboard. The last icon generates a random password automatically. Enter a date under “Expiry date” to set a time limit for the link. Once the date has expired, the link is no longer accessible. If you do not enter a date, the link will not expire.

Once all options have been set, click on “Copy link” to generate the public link. The link is now created and can be shared with the recipients. Now you know how you can share files and folders securely and individually with external users.
Role view download upload edit add delete manage members disable / enable Space edit quota delete Space can view x x - - - - - - - - can edit x x x x x x - - - - can manage x x x x x x x x x x
In a Space, members can have different roles that give them different levels of access.
This role allows the member to view and download files in the Space, but they cannot make any changes, upload or create new files and folders.

With this role, the member can do everything a "Can View" member can do, plus: Upload files to the Space Create new files and folders Delete files and folders, including their history Restore deleted files

This role gives the member all the abilities of "Can Edit," plus: Add or remove members from the Space Change the roles of other members in the Space Enable and disable the Space Edit the quota of the Space Delete the Space Each role determines what a member can do within the Space!

The “File Drop” allows people who receive a specific link (the so-called “link recipients”) to upload files to a shared folder. The trick: The “link recipients” cannot see the files already in the folder.
documents.s: File Drop (secret) - This is how it works

documents.t: Click on the “three dot menu” next to the file or folder name you want to share. Select “Share” from the drop-down menu. A sidebar window will open on the right-hand side of the screen. At the sidebar window, you will find the area for “Public links”. There click on the "Add link" button. Select the option for "Secret File Drop". Type a password and confirm the entries with click on "copy link". You can also set an "Expiry date", if you want the link to expire. External Sharing, if you want to know how to set the Expiry date. Now you send the link and the password to a "link recipient".

The “link recipient” opens the received link in a browser. The “link recipient” enters the password and then clicks on “Continue”. Upload files: The “File drop folder” is opened. The “link recipient” can upload their files here without being able to view the existing contents of the folder. As confirmation that the upload was successful, a pop-up window appears in the lower right corner. important The “link recipient” does not have access to existing files - they can only add new files. Now you know how you can share a file drop link

You can easily invite new members to collaborate within your Space by following the steps below. Go to your Space and open the members' menu. In the members menu, you can search for the user from your organization that you want to add. Select the desired rights for the member, e.g. whether the person may only read or also edit. Click on “Add” to invite the member to the Space. To change the rights of a member, open the members menu, click on the corresponding user and select the desired new rights. Open the members menu, click on the three-dot menu next to the member you want to remove and select “Remove member”. note The removed member immediately loses access to the space. important The added member has immediate access to the Space. Attention: Every user with the “can manage” right has the authorization to deactivate and delete the Space. A deleted Space cannot be restored, so be careful who you grant these rights to. Now you know how to add members to your Space and set the permissions accordingly!
documents.s: Add a new member to your Space in OpenCloud

documents.t: Spaces are collaborative areas meant to be used by multiple users. Unlike personal storage, they must be structured in a way that supports clarity, collaboration, and scalability. This guide helps you set up and maintain well-organized, long-term usable Spaces.
documents.s: Best Practices for Organizing Spaces in OpenCloud

documents.t: Plan first – Don't treat Spaces like ad-hoc storage. Think ahead. Think in roles and teams – Structure based on how people work together. Keep it scalable – Choose a structure that works now and with more users later. Apply consistency – Naming, access, and structure should follow shared rules.
📁 Family Space ├── 📂 Documents │ ├── 🧾 Insurance │ └── 📑 Contracts ├── 📂 Photos │ ├── 📸 2024 │ └── 📸 2023 └── 📂 Shared Notes

📁 2024 ├── 📂 Class 3B │ ├── 📂 Teaching Materials │ ├── 📂 Parent Communication │ ├── 📂 Homework Submissions │ └── 📂 Events & Photos ├── 📂 Class 4C │ ├── 📂 Teaching Materials │ ├── 📂 Parent Communication │ ├── 📂 Homework Submissions │ └── 📂 Events & Photos
📁 Marketing Team ├── 📂 Campaigns │ ├── 📂 Q1-2025 │ └── 📂 Q2-2025 ├── 📂 Templates ├── 📂 Reports └── 📂 Meeting Notes

Use clear, descriptive names – avoid "new folder" or cryptic titles Prefer lowercase-with-dashes or Title Case Add dates when relevant: report-2025-Q2.pdf or Budget 2024.xlsx Avoid special characters: & % $ § ! may break integrations

Assign Space Owners: Responsible for structure and permissions Use Groups where possible for access control (e.g. staff, students, parents) Keep sensitive content in separate folders with restricted access Define editing vs. viewing rights clearly
Set up an archive folder for old or unused files Annually review the Space and remove outdated content Use versioning or export before deletion if unsure

❌ Don’t ✅ Instead Dump all files in root folder Use clear subfolders Mix personal and shared content Keep personal data in "Personal" Give all users full access Apply least-privilege principle Use inconsistent naming Define and follow conventions
You can use this as a template for new Spaces: 📁 [Team/Project Name] ├── 📂 Documents ├── 📂 Planning ├── 📂 Resources ├── 📂 Archive └── README.md (Space purpose, structure, rules)

Goal How Make Spaces easy to navigate Use clear folder names & hierarchy Avoid permission chaos Define ownership and roles Keep things clean Review regularly and archive Support collaboration Use group access & standard naming
With Spaces you can share data with specific people within your organization. All members of a Space have the defined access to the data, and you can customize the editing rights for each member.
documents.s: Create Spaces and share data in OpenCloud

documents.t: Go to the menu item "Spaces” in the left sidebar Click on "+ New Space” Enter the desired name for the Space. Click on "Create”.

Your new Space now appears under "Spaces” and is ready to be used Now you know how to quickly and easily create a Space and can start sharing data! important Spaces can only be permanently shared within your own organization.

Here are the steps to share files or folders with other members or groups internally Click on the “three dot menu” next to the file or folder name you want to share. Select “Share” from the drop-down menu. A sidebar window will open on the right-hand side of the screen. At the top of the sidebar window, you will find the area for “Internal sharing”. Search for individual members or entire groups by entering them in the search bar. Select the people or groups with whom you would like to share the folder or file. Select the rights for the selected members or groups: “Can view": Members can only view and download the file, but cannot edit it or upload it to shared folders. “Can upload": Members can view and download the file and upload their own files to the shared folder. “Can edit": Members have the same rights as in "can upload", but can also create new files, edit existing files and delete them. Click on “Share” to confirm the selection. The shared members or groups are listed under “shared with” in the same window. Now you know how you share files and folders within your organization and manage access accordingly.
documents.s: Organization internal sharing of files and folders in OpenCloud

documents.t: You can customize your Space in two places In the Space overview In the Space itself
Click on the “Context menu” in the Space overview or directly in the Space.

Click "Edit Description." Enter your description into the text editor that opens, then save and close it. The description of the space can also be changed directly in the space by clicking on the pencil next to the description.
Click "Edit subtitle". Enter the desired subtitle into the opened text editor and save.
Click "Edit image" and "set image". In the window that opens, select the desired image for your space and confirm. Now select the desired section and confirm.
Click "Edit image" and "Set Icon". In the window that opens, select the desired icon for your space. Now you know how you can design and customize the Space to your liking!
A Space can be disabled and later enabled in various ways. This function can only be performed by a Space Admin or the Administrator.
documents.s: Disable and Enable a Space in OpenCloud

documents.t: Select the desired Space. Click on Disable in the top taskbar. Alternatively, you can click on the three dots menu in the Space overview and select Disable from the menu. Go directly to the Space. There, click on the three dots menu and select Disable. Confirm the deactivation. note No data will be deleted, but only the Space Admin or the Administrator can enable the Space.

After the Space has been disabled, the Enable option now appears in place of Disable. Click on it to enable the Space. If the Space is not visible, the filter Show disabled Spaces may be enabled. Click on the filter and the Space will appear so that it can be enabled.

In the Administration settings under Spaces, the Administrator can also enable the Space. The Administrator clicks on the three dots menu and selects Enable. Confirm the selection by clicking on Enable. The Space is now displayed again in the Space Overview. Now you know how you can disable a Space and enable it again if required.

You can rename an existing Space at any time to better reflect its purpose or content. Click on the three dots menu or do a right click on the Space in the Space Overview and select Rename Enter the new name for the Space and confirm. Now your Space is renamed.
Before you can delete a Space, it must first be disabled. Here are the steps you should follow:

Follow the instructions for Disable a Space to set the Space to the deactivated state before deleting it. If the Space is no longer visible after deactivation, click on the Include disabled filter to display it again.

Select the Space you want to delete. Click on Delete in the top bar, or open the three-dot menu and select Delete Confirm that you are sure that you want to delete the space caution A deleted Space cannot be restored. It is permanently and irretrievably deleted. Therefore, be careful to whom you grant can manage rights for your Space. Please remember to ensure that no important data is lost before deleting. Now you know how to delete a space.
As a Space administrator, you can adjust the storage capacity (quota) of a Space to increase or decrease the available storage space as required.
documents.s: Edit the quota of a Space

documents.t: Select the Edit quota option in the Top-Bar or in the Context-Menu Two options are available in the window that opens: Select a default size (e.g. 5 GB) from the drop-down menu. Enter an individual storage size. Click on Confirm to apply the change. The quota of the space is updated according to the new settings.
documents.s: Steps to edit the Space quota

documents.t: Alan creates a folder called "Project" and shares it with several Persons Later, Alan leaves the organization As Alan's account is deleted, some shared folder disappears User loses access to some folders and their content

Potential data loss when the original owner leaves Project data counts toward personal quotas, which is not ideal for teamwork Administrative overhead when ownership needs to be transferred Spaghetti sharing chaos – users get lost in their "Shared with me" or "Shared with others" sections

Spaces are designed to overcome these limitations and provide a more stable, team-friendly solution Organization-owned: Spaces belong to the organization, not individuals. Collaborative management: Multiple users can manage a Space together. Dedicated quota: Each Space has its own storage limit, separate from personal quotas. note Spaces make collaboration simpler, safer, and more scalable — especially in professional environments.
